sourceMovie.title: Mortdecai
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mortdecai (2015) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Koepp's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lost City
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mortdecai clicked for you, The Lost City (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mortdecai?
faq.answer: Try The Lost City, Romancing the Stone, Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway—each shares DNA with Mortdecai while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mortdecai?
faq.answer: Mortdecai (2015) is best described as Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mortdecai worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
